title: Define Workers
breadcrumbs: Deployment Considerations for Performance and Scalability
text: Define Workers A worker is a Tomcat process that accepts work from the web server. You need to create a workers.properties file that defines the workers. For the Apache Web Server, create the worker.properties file in the ApacheInstallDirectory /Apache/conf directory. For Microsoft IIS, create the worker.properties file in the Tomcat installation that is working with IIS Redirector, for example, /tomcat/conf . Define two or more workers in the file. For example: # Simple workers configuration file # Check the JK Documentation of Tomcat 4 for more information # ps=/ # List all workers worker.list=tomcat1,tomcat2,loadbalancer # Redefining the workers of type ajp13 worker.tomcat1.port=11009 worker.tomcat1.host=Tomcat_server1 worker.tomcat1.type=ajp13 worker.tomcat1.lbfactor=100 worker.tomcat2.port=12009 worker.tomcat2.host=Tomcat_server2 worker.tomcat2.type=ajp13 worker.tomcat2.lbfactor=100 # Specify the size of the open connection cache. worker.tomcat1.cachesize # Specify the load balance factor when used with # a load balancing worker. worker.loadbalancer.type=lb worker.loadbalancer.balanced_workers=tomcat1,tomcat2 Copy For IIS, generate uriworkermap.properties to mount Uniface servlet and redirect it to the loadbalancer worker. # Simple uri worker map configuration file # Check the JK Documentation of Tomcat 4 for more information # default.worker=loadbalancer uniface.worker=loadbalancer # Mount the Uniface context /uniface/servlet/*.*=$(uniface.worker) /uniface/*=$(uniface.worker) Copy Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
